message.content:
Excellent! This is a ray tracer. I can see key function names: `sky_color`, `sphere_intersect`, `plane_intersect`, `is_in_shadow`, `ray_create`, `ray_at`, `vector_normalize`, `write_image`. Let me study the output image more and try to decompile it.

{"analysis":"The mystery binary is a ray tracer that renders an 800x600 PPM image with spheres and planes. Key functions identified from strings: sky_color, sphere_intersect, plane_intersect, is_in_shadow, ray_create, ray_at, vector_normalize. I need to understand the scene layout, camera setup, and rendering algorithm precisely.","plan":"1. Examine different parts of the output image (middle rows, bottom) to understand the scene
2. Try to disassemble or decompile the binary to get exact parameters
3. Check if objdump is available for disassembly","commands":[{"keystrokes":"sed -n '301,305p' /app/image.ppm
","duration":0.1},{"keystrokes":"tail -c 100 /app/image.ppm
","duration":0.1},{"keystrokes":"sed -n '603,610p' /app/image.ppm
","duration":0.1},{"keystrokes":"objdump -d /app/mystery 2>/dev/null | head -20
","duration":1},{"keystrokes":"which objdump 2>/dev/null; which readelf 2>/dev/null; which nm 2>/dev/null; which gdb 2>/dev/null; which python3 2>/dev/null
","duration":0.1}]}
